Elias Range Stays

Our Story

Why we exist. What we’re building. Where we’re going.

Our Why
The people keeping our hospitals open
deserve better than a hotel room.
The real crisis isn’t staffing numbers — it’s what happens to the people filling those roles.

The healthcare staffing crisis isn’t just about empty positions on a roster. It’s about what happens to the people filling them. Nurses and doctors who drive hours to rural postings, show up for grueling shifts, then come home to a hotel room with no community, no roots, no way to recover. People who give everything to their patients and have nothing waiting for them at the end of the day.

60% of Canadian healthcare professionals are burned out. 80% feel isolated. They’re not being filled back up — and eventually, they leave.

Housing is the #1 barrier to rural healthcare recruitment. But it’s more than logistics. Where you live shapes whether you stay. Whether you thrive. Whether you show up tomorrow ready to give again.

ERS was built to change that — from the ground up, in the communities that need it most. We are not a hotel alternative. We are not a property manager. We are an accommodations and community platform: purpose-built housing near hospitals, 24/7 support, and a community experience that makes people feel genuinely taken care of.

We’re grassroots. Community-connected, locally embedded — starting in Northern BC, where the gap is widest and the need is most acute. Health authorities don’t want another corporate chain. They want a partner rooted in the same communities their staff serve.

60%
Healthcare workers burned out
80%
Feel isolated on posting
#1
Housing = top recruitment barrier

“The hotels have tried to work with us. We don’t want to work with hotels.”

— Senior Health Authority Executive, Northern BC
Our Mission
To serve those who provide health to others.
Simple. Unambiguous. Everything we build flows from this.
Our Vision
A future where those who care for others
are cared for in return.
Not just a place to sleep — a community that fills people back up.

A future where every nurse, doctor, technician, and construction worker building the next generation of hospitals can arrive somewhere and feel at home — housed, supported, and connected to the community around them.

ERS is building the full-stack infrastructure layer for healthcare workforce housing in Canada. Not just a place to sleep. A community platform — purpose-built housing near hospitals, rooted in BC’s natural landscapes, with services, amenities, and programs that make people genuinely thrive.

🏗

Infrastructure & Accommodations

Purpose-built housing near hospitals. Fully furnished, move-in ready, turnkey managed. The physical backbone that keeps healthcare staffed.

🏔

Healthy Living & Outdoor

BC’s landscape is part of the platform. Outdoor excursions, trails, nature — the recovery and recharge that healthcare workers need to thrive.

🤝

Community & Connection

Long table dinners, hosted experiences, local partnerships. Fighting isolation by building real belonging — the Draw Close program.

Our model serves both sides: the construction companies who build the hospitals, and the health authorities who staff them. As we grow, ERS hubs become anchors — development clusters around hospitals that drive retention, community health outcomes, and local economic vitality across British Columbia and beyond.

This is what Healthy Living, Stronger Communities means in practice. Not just less cost — more retention, more stability, more community health, and more meaningful lives for the people keeping our healthcare system alive.

“Not just less — more. More retention. More stability. More community health outcomes. More meaning for the people who show up every day to keep our hospitals open.”
